subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV/PCI, World Bank, EC, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem, Freedom House) + independent/regional press (Balkan Insight/BIRN, Al Jazeera, Euronews, RFE/RL); heavy pro-government media capture is documented, so monitors are weighted over any official narrative.
An EU-candidate experiencing democratic backsliding: a live legitimacy crisis after the 2024 Novi Sad station-canopy collapse triggered the largest protests in decades.

Standout drags: institutional capture, an active protest/legitimacy crisis, Western-unreliable hedging (no Russia sanctions), and steep demographic decline; sound macro is the offset. Source-lean: monitors over captured domestic media.

economic.notes: The cleanest category - prudent fiscals, a credible managed dinar, and sound banks.

fiscal_state.notes: Debt below ~48% GDP and a deficit ~2% (under the ceiling), IMF-anchored. (IMF 2025 Art IV/PCI)

monetary_independence.notes: Managed dinar-euro stability with a credible central bank and high reserves - well-run if not fully independent. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

inflation.notes: Eased to ~4% within the band. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

banking_stability.notes: Well-capitalised, liquid, with recovered NPLs. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Autos/manufacturing and EU-integrated nearshoring, with a big Chinese FDI bet and a contested lithium-mining project as uncertain upside. (US State Dept ICS; EC)

institutional.notes: Clearly negative - compromised courts and heavily captured media.

rule_of_law.notes: Compromised judicial independence, appointment capture, and prosecutors pressured after probing cabinet abuses. (EC 2025 Rule of Law Report; TI)

statistical_integrity.notes: Usable and EU-engaged; the IMF works with the data. (IMF Art IV)

press_freedom.notes: A downward spiral - record attacks on journalists at 2025 protests, spyware, and pro-government tabloid/ad-subsidy capture. (RSF 2025; Freedom House)

political_social.notes: A live legitimacy crisis and steep demographic decline.

political_stability.notes: Competitive-authoritarian dominance facing the largest protests in decades since the Novi Sad canopy collapse killed 16; the government refuses snap elections - durable but contested. (Euronews; Al Jazeera)

civil_liberties.notes: Protest crackdowns, spyware, smear campaigns, and retaliation against critics. (Freedom House 2026; Amnesty)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Lawful routes, a transit country with some Russian/Chinese inflows - moderate. (UNECE)

social_cohesion.notes: Polarised, with Kosovo-grievance nationalism and a protest-driven society/regime cleavage. (CEPS)

demographics.notes: A steep decline (down ~12% since 1990), a median age over 45, and heavy medic emigration. (UNDP; OSW; World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: An EU-candidate that hedges Russia/China and carries an unresolved Kosovo dispute.

alliance_reliability.notes: An EU-candidate that refuses to sanction Russia and hedges EU/Russia/China - unreliable to the West. (Euronews; RFE/RL)

conflict_involvement.notes: No active war but an unresolved Kosovo dispute with periodic flare-ups. (ECIPE; RFE/RL)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: An open economy, not sanctioned, broadly EU-aligned on capital. (US State Dept ICS)

physical_practical.notes: Improving infrastructure, against severe coal-driven air pollution and medic emigration.

climate.notes: Balkan/Pannonian heat and drought on agriculture, plus river flooding, low-moderate readiness. (ND-GAIN; EEA)

healthcare.notes: Universal-ish but uneven, with heavy medic emigration. (GMF; Balkan Insight)

infrastructure.notes: Improving via EU/China funds, though the canopy collapse exposed quality/oversight risk. (ECIPE)

crime_safety.notes: Moderate, with an organized-crime undercurrent. (GI-TOC; UNODC)

summary: An EU-candidate with quietly sound macro fundamentals - prudent fiscals, a credibly managed dinar, low inflation, and well-capitalised banks - but those are outweighed by democratic backsliding. Institutions are captured (compromised courts, pressured prosecutors, heavily controlled media), and a live legitimacy crisis is underway: the 2024 Novi Sad station-canopy collapse that killed 16 triggered the largest protests in decades, met with crackdowns and a refusal to call elections. Belgrade refuses to sanction Russia and hedges between the EU, Russia, and China, the Kosovo dispute is unresolved, air pollution is severe, and the population is in steep demographic decline.
